records.ety: [L. ex out + culpatus, p. p. of culpare to find fault with, to blame, culpa fault. See Culpable.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Exculpated (?), p. pr. & vb. n.. Exculpating (<?/).]

defs: To clear from alleged fault or guilt; to prove to be guiltless; to relieve of blame; to acquit.
quotes: He exculpated himself from being the author of the heroic epistle.
I exculpate him further for his writing against me.
